summary: Remastered soundboard capture of a scorching 1972 show, highlighted by an epic Truckin' → The Other One → Morning Dew jam and clean, though slightly bass‑heavy, audio.
review: Fans rave about the incendiary second‑set stretch—Truckin', The Other One and Morning Dew—calling it one of the band’s most intense jams, with Phil’s bass solo singled out as a masterpiece. The Bertha soundboard source is praised for its clarity and hot remastering, despite minor bass‑heavy coloration and pitch quirks on the final tracks.

ai_rating.rationale: Average reviewer rating is ~4.5/5; overwhelmingly positive comments about both performance (standout jams, inventive bass) and recording quality (clean remaster, minor issues only).

recording_quality.technical_notes: Clean, well‑remastered Bertha tape; a touch overly bassy and a few pitch discrepancies on the closing tracks. Some listeners note occasional cuts in the Truckin’/Other One segment but overall clarity is high.

show_quality.setlist_flow: First set is solid but uneven, building to a spectacular 2½‑hour second set where post‑drums jams lift the band into high gear; the Truckin’ → Other One → Dew sequence serves as the show’s climax.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Garcia’s hot tone and soaring leads are repeatedly praised, though his mix is a bit low on ‘Big River’. His solo on Morning Dew receives special kudos.
band_member_comments.Phil: Phil’s bass solo in the jam is called one of his most inventive; his partnership with Bill in the drum‑and‑bass segment gets high marks.
band_member_comments.Keys: Keyboard work (Keith/Brent) is noted for joining the post‑drums jam and adding texture, while overall keyboard balance is considered good.
band_member_comments.Drums: Bill’s drumming drives the epic jams; his interplay with Phil in the drum‑and‑bass section is highlighted as a highlight.
